title: Air-fryer chicken shawarma
subtitle: Marinated chicken thighs with cumin, coriander, and turmeric
excerpt: Boneless chicken thighs marinated in cumin, coriander, smoked paprika, turmeric, garlic, and lemon, then cooked in the air fryer at 200°C for 20 minutes. Rest and slice thinly before serving. The air fryer produces the slightly charred edges that define the dish without a rotisserie spit.
sourceNotes: Shawarma is a Levantine street food made from stacked layers of meat cooked on a vertical spit. The home version uses chicken thighs marinated in the same spice blend and cooked flat, then sliced to replicate the thin-shaved result. The air fryer produces slight charring on the edges that a standard oven does not, making it a closer approximation of the original than a tray-baked version.

content.text: Rest the cooked chicken for at least 5 minutes before slicing. The thighs will be flat and whole from the basket: the rest firms them enough to slice thinly without shredding. Thin slices are the point: they replicate the shaved-from-a-spit texture and absorb the garlic sauce more effectively than thick chunks.

Mix the {{cumin-ground}}, {{coriander-ground}}, {{smoked-paprika}}, {{turmeric}}, {{garlic}}, {{lemon}} juice, {{olive-oil}}, and {{sea-salt-fine}} in a bowl. Add the {{chicken-thighs-boneless}} and toss to coat. Marinate for at least 1 hour (or up to 24 hours in the fridge). Preheat the air fryer to 200°C for 3 minutes. Cook the chicken in a single layer at 200°C for 20 minutes, flipping halfway, until cooked through and charred at the edges. Rest for 5 minutes, then slice thinly against the grain. Serve in flatbreads with garlic sauce, tomato, and pickled chilli.

items.fix: Preheat thoroughly and shake excess marinade from each thigh before cooking. A very wet surface steams rather than chars. A final 2 minutes on the highest setting will add colour
items.cause: Air fryer not preheated, or chicken very wet from marinade

items.fix: Pull at 74°C internal and rest for 5 minutes before slicing. Thin slices stay moister than thick chunks because the meat has a higher surface-area-to-volume ratio and the marinade coats more of each piece
A 1-hour marinade is the minimum. Overnight is noticeably better. The acidic lemon juice also helps the spices penetrate the meat

content.text: Air-fryer chicken shawarma is a weeknight dinner that tastes like a takeaway. Serve in wraps or flatbreads with garlic sauce, salad, and pickled chilli. The marinated chicken can go straight from the fridge into the air fryer if it was prepped the night before.
